title: Slide visual redesign — make slides look actually good
description: Apply the two polished template designs from TASK-028 (Geometric Deco dark + Architectural Editorial light) to the in-app slide preview. Pure CSS + component work — update slides.css and SlideRenderer.tsx to match the visual quality of the standalone templates in docs/slide_deck_templates/. Key challenge: make the designs flexible enough to handle variable user content (different bullet counts, text lengths, stat counts) without breaking layout.
acceptance_criteria: Slides look visually impressive enough to screenshot and post on X
Title slides have strong visual presence (gradient/color bg, bold typography)
Content slides use cards/containers with depth, not just floating text on white
Code slides have polished dark theme treatment
Metrics slides have visually striking number presentation
Closing slides feel like a strong ending, not an afterthought
Multiple theme options that all look professional
Slides render well at 16:9 aspect ratio
notes: NEEDS PLANNING. Design research done in TASK-028 — two templates finalized (Geometric Deco dark + Architectural Editorial light) covering all 5 slide types. Direction approved by Beckham. Next: plan the CSS + component changes to bring the template visuals into the in-app preview. Key flexibility issues to solve: content slides need to handle N bullets (not just 3 horizontal cards), metrics grid needs to reflow for 2-5 stats, subtitle white-space:nowrap will clip long text. No Reveal.js integration — that's TASK-026 scope.
